_meta.description: V1 Anomaly Tracker — Ticker Universe & News Source Config

kronos_input.notes: Each ticker needs a pandas DataFrame of OHLCV candles + timestamp Series. Use predict_batch() to parallelize.

finnhub.coverage: Major wires — Bloomberg, Reuters, MarketWatch, CNBC, WSJ, SEC filings
finnhub.best_for: Large/mid-cap US equities with strong institutional coverage
finnhub.limitation: Weak on small-caps, no international non-English sources

gdelt.rate_limit: Rate-limited but no published hard cap — use 1-2 sec delays between calls
gdelt.coverage: 250K+ global outlets, 152 languages machine-translated to English, broadcast TV via Internet Archive
gdelt.best_for: Geopolitical/macro events, international coverage, sentiment tone scoring, catching catalysts Finnhub misses
gdelt.limitation: No ticker-native search — must search by company name. 250 article cap per query. 3-month rolling window only.

search_strategy.method: Search by company name OR brand, not ticker symbol

example_queries.AAPL: Apple iPhone OR iPad OR Tim Cook

search_strategy.tone_filter: Use toneabs>5 to surface emotionally-charged coverage (likely catalysts)

news_sources.pipeline_logic: Finnhub fires FIRST (fast, structured JSON). If Finnhub returns <3 articles, GDELT fires as fallback with broader keyword search. For macro tickers (ETFs, commodities), GDELT fires FIRST since Finnhub has no coverage for these.

tier_1_bellwethers.description: Mega/Large Cap — S&P 100 core + key additions. Highest news coverage, most liquid, lowest noise.

tier_2_high_beta.description: High-volatility, high-momentum names. Most likely to generate anomaly signals. Includes meme stocks, recent IPOs, and speculative growth.

tier_3_sector_etfs.description: Sector and thematic ETFs. Essential for detecting rotations and macro-driven moves. GDELT is primary news source here since Finnhub doesn't cover ETFs well.
tier_3_sector_etfs.gdelt_search_keywords: Use thematic keywords, not ETF names

tier_4_macro_indicators.description: Macro barometers — bonds, commodities, currencies, volatility. These are your early-warning system for regime changes. GDELT is primary since these move on geopolitical/macro news.

tickers.name: iShares 20+ Year Treasury ETF
tickers.gdelt_query: treasury bonds interest rates Federal Reserve

tickers.name: iShares High Yield Corporate Bond ETF
Invesco CurrencyShares Japanese Yen
tickers.gdelt_query: Japanese yen Bank of Japan currency

tickers.name: Invesco CurrencyShares British Pound
tickers.gdelt_query: British pound sterling Bank of England

tickers.name: iShares J.P. Morgan USD Emerging Markets Bond

tier_5_small_cap_movers.description: High-volume small/mid-caps with outsized move potential. Finnhub coverage is spotty here, so GDELT becomes critical. Consider alert-on-volatility-only as fallback when news is unavailable.
tier_5_small_cap_movers.fallback_strategy: If both Finnhub AND GDELT return 0 articles, still alert on volatility signal alone with note: 'No catalyst identified — check SEC filings'

finnhub.rate_strategy: 60 req/min. Budget: ~5-10 anomaly tickers per cycle max. Queue by sigma magnitude descending.

gdelt.auth: None — completely free, no API key
gdelt.rate_strategy: No published limit but rate-limited under load. Use 1-2 sec delay between calls. Limit to ~10-15 calls per 15-min cycle.
gdelt.tone_scoring: Built-in. Returns avg tone per article. Use toneabs>5 to filter for high-emotion coverage likely to be market-moving.
gdelt.multilingual: Searches across 65 machine-translated languages — catches international catalysts Finnhub misses entirely
query_tips: Search by company name, NOT ticker symbol
Use OR for brand variants: 'Apple iPhone OR iPad OR MacBook'
Add sourcecountry:US to reduce noise for domestic equities
Use near10: operator for precision: near10:'Tesla recall'
timespan=60min for recent, timespan=24h for broader context

anomaly_detected_flow: 1. Kronos flags ticker with sigma > threshold
3. Fire primary source (Finnhub for equities, GDELT for ETFs/macro)
4. If primary returns <3 articles, fire secondary source
5. Merge and deduplicate articles by URL
6. Pass merged payload to Narrator (Groq/Gemini) for synthesis
7. Include GDELT tone score in Discord alert if available
gdelt_bonus_features: Tone timeline: Track sentiment shift over 24h for flagged ticker
Volume spike detection: If GDELT article volume for a company jumps 3x vs baseline, flag even without Kronos anomaly
Cross-language alerts: International coverage appearing before US market open = potential alpha
